purpose: Reload configuration from /etc/sonic/config_db.json into running CONFIG_DB (Redis), restarting affected services
prerequisites: Verify the config file is valid JSON: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('/etc/sonic/config_db.json'))"
Understand that unsaved running config changes will be LOST -- config reload replaces Redis with the file
Plan for brief traffic disruption as services restart
warnings: config reload replaces the running CONFIG_DB with the file. If you made changes and did NOT run 'config save', those changes will be lost.
All *mgrd daemons will restart, causing brief service interruption
BGP sessions will flap as bgpcfgd re-renders FRR config
LAG/PortChannel may briefly flap as teammgrd restarts

steps.notes: Always backup before reload. This captures the RUNNING config, not the file.

steps.action: Validate the config file to be loaded
steps.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/etc/sonic/config_db.json')); print(f'Valid JSON: {len(d)} top-level keys')"

verification.confirms_if: sonic-cfggen can parse the config file

steps.notes: Catch JSON syntax errors before they cause a failed reload

verification.confirms_if: Config was loaded successfully into Redis

steps.warning: This restarts multiple daemons. BGP will flap. Expect 10-30 seconds of disruption.

verification.command: show bgp summary; show interfaces status | head -20
verification.confirms_if: All critical services are running and functional

steps.notes: BGP may take 30-60 seconds to fully reconverge depending on number of peers and routes

rollback.action: Restore previous running config from backup
rollback.notes: Replace <timestamp> with the backup file you created in step 1
